name: SciPy
what_it_does: SciPy provides scientific algorithms for optimization, statistics, signal processing, linear algebra, and sparse matrix operations built on top of NumPy.
use_cases: Run numerical optimization (minimize, curve_fit) to fit models or find parameter optima
Perform statistical hypothesis tests (t-test, chi-squared, ANOVA) and compute distributions
Apply signal processing filters (butterworth, FFT, convolution) to time-series sensor data
Solve sparse linear systems efficiently using scipy.sparse for large graph or FEM problems
Compute spatial distances, interpolations, or integration over irregular grids
not_for: Deep learning or gradient-based ML model training (use PyTorch or JAX instead)
Symbolic algebraic manipulation or exact closed-form solutions (use SymPy instead)
Real-time or streaming signal processing with sub-millisecond latency requirements
best_when: You need battle-tested scientific algorithms (stats, optimization, signal, sparse) without implementing them from scratch.
avoid_when: Your problem requires GPU acceleration, symbolic math, or distributed computation.

auth.notes: Pure Python library, no authentication required.

pricing.notes: Free and open source under BSD license.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Optimization convergence failures return status codes rather than exceptions — agents must check OptimizeResult.success and message fields explicitly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All functions are pure and stateless; repeated calls with same inputs return same results.
known_agent_gotchas: scipy.optimize functions return an OptimizeResult object — success=False does not raise an exception, so agents must explicitly check result.success before using result.x
Sparse matrix format matters significantly: CSR is efficient for row slicing and matrix-vector products, COO for construction, CSC for column operations — wrong format causes silent 10-100x slowdowns
LAPACK/BLAS backend must be present; importing scipy on a minimal environment without openblas or MKL will raise ImportError or produce incorrect results silently
scipy.stats distribution methods (pdf, cdf, ppf) accept loc and scale but not mean/std directly — agents often pass wrong parameter names causing silent wrong results
scipy.signal filter design (butter, cheby) returns coefficients (b, a) that must then be passed to sosfilt or lfilter — skipping the second step is a common agent mistake that produces no error

security_score_components.security_notes: No network surface; depends on numpy and BLAS/LAPACK native libraries — supply chain hygiene of C extension dependencies is the primary concern.
